evals.name: build-login-form
evals.prompt: Create a login form with email and password fields, including validation and a submit button.

assertions.text: Uses v-form with ref and @submit.prevent
Uses v-text-field with variant='outlined' (or relies on project defaults)
Includes validation rules (required, email format)
Uses @mdi/js icons (mdiEmail, mdiLock) for field icons
Uses v-btn with color='primary' for submit
Calls form.value.validate() before submitting
All user-visible text uses t() for i18n

evals.name: data-table-pagination
evals.prompt: Build an admin users table with server-side pagination, sorting, and action buttons for edit and delete.

assertions.text: Uses v-data-table-server (not client-side v-data-table)
Defines headers array with title, key, sortable properties
Implements v-model:options with page, itemsPerPage, sortBy
Passes items-length for total count
Uses custom slot (#item.actions) for edit/delete buttons
Imports icons from @mdi/js (mdiPencil, mdiDelete)
Shows loading state while fetching data

evals.name: icon-usage
evals.prompt: How do I use icons in this project? I want to add a search icon to a text field and a plus icon to a button.

assertions.text: Imports from @mdi/js: mdiMagnify and mdiPlus
Uses :prepend-inner-icon or :append-inner-icon for v-text-field
Uses :prepend-icon or :icon for v-btn
Does NOT use string class names like 'mdi-magnify'
Mentions https://pictogrammers.com/library/mdi/ for finding icons

evals.name: confirm-dialog
evals.prompt: Create a reusable confirmation dialog that I can use for delete confirmations across the app.

assertions.text: Uses v-dialog with v-model and max-width
Sets persistent prop to prevent outside-click dismiss
Uses v-card with title, text, and actions slots
Provides cancel and confirm buttons
Uses Promise-based pattern or defineExpose for parent usage
Accepts color parameter for destructive actions (color='error')
All text uses t() for i18n

evals.name: theming-dark-mode
evals.prompt: How do I toggle between light and dark theme in the app? What colors are available?

assertions.text: Uses useTheme() composable from vuetify
Toggles theme.global.name.value between 'light' and 'dark'
Lists project color tokens: primary (#2563eb), secondary (#7c3aed), accent (#06b6d4)
Mentions dark theme overrides surface/background/on-* colors
Explains colors are used via color='primary' prop, never hardcoded hex

evals.name: responsive-navigation
evals.prompt: Create a responsive layout with a side navigation that's a temporary drawer on mobile and a rail on desktop.

assertions.text: Uses useDisplay() composable from vuetify to detect mobile
Uses v-navigation-drawer with :temporary='mobile' and :rail='!mobile'
Optionally uses expand-on-hover for rail mode
Includes v-list with v-list-item for nav items
Uses v-app-bar-nav-icon or button to toggle drawer
Icons imported from @mdi/js

evals.name: snackbar-notification
evals.prompt: Add a toast notification that shows success or error messages after form submissions.

assertions.text: Uses v-snackbar with v-model
Sets location prop (e.g., 'bottom end')
Sets timeout (e.g., 4000ms)
Uses color prop for success/error variants
Provides a function to trigger the snackbar with custom text and color
Includes close button via #actions slot

evals.name: card-with-loading
evals.prompt: Create a card component that shows a skeleton loader while data is being fetched, then displays content.

assertions.text: Uses v-skeleton-loader with appropriate type (e.g., card-heading, text)
Conditionally renders skeleton vs content with v-if/v-else
Uses v-card for the content container
Card uses project defaults (rounded xl, no elevation, border)
May use the v-card :loading prop with v-progress-linear
